textPlain: California is ahead of the curve:DNA of every baby born in California is stored. Who has access to it? - CBS News https://www.cbsnews.com/news/california-biobank-dna-babies-w...

DHS served me a warrant a few years ago to "search my body"The initial complainant was an anonymous officer handling an anonymous dog (neverminded it's against CBP policy to use dogs on people rather than property, and what was described never happened).  That is, some guy just told the judge some anonymous guy's anonymous dog accused me of having drugs.   And the warrant was retroactive for something they already did.  Total insanity.
I liked the world better when the tinfoil hat people were wrong.
Have they ever encouraged people to submit DNA samples in order to identify criminal relatives?  I could see this going over well with the law-and-order part of the population.

The irony is that they didn't force people to give their DNA (except if you were arrested) but instead people willingly gave it and even paid for it.If you think Bill Gates is going to implant a chip in your brain via a COVID vaccine you are mistaken. Once such tech exists (if ever) people will be lining up in queues and pay a lot of money to get it implanted.
So back before we came out of the trees?
This is a great call, these folks aren't devious or clever enough to understand that they can just ask...
